bid_claims.quote_text: Mr. Fortnum suggested that a price of $17.00 per share on a fully diluted basis could likely get support from Ingredion’s board.
providing an indicative valuation of $18.00 per share in cash for all of the outstanding capital stock of Penford on a fully diluted basis.
Ingredion could potentially increase its proposal from $18.00 to $18.25 or $18.50 per share on a fully diluted basis.
Mr. Fortnum then stated that Ingredion was prepared to move forward based on a proposed price of $18.50 per share on a fully diluted basis.
Penford received a letter from Ingredion regarding its indication of interest and increasing the proposed price to $19.00 per share of outstanding Penford capital stock on a fully diluted basis.
Party A indicated any offer would be below $17.50 - $18.00 per share in cash.
its value range for a potential transaction had been reduced from $17.50 - $18.00 per share to $16.00 - $18.00 per share
Party A provided a formal letter with its indication of interest with respect to an acquisition of the Company at a price of $16.00 per share

event_claims.description: Ingredion's CEO advised Penford's CEO of Ingredion's interest in acquiring Penford.
event_claims.quote_text: On July 17, 2014, Mr. Malkoski and Ms. Gordon met for lunch, during which meeting Ms. Gordon advised Mr. Malkoski of Ingredion’s interest in acquiring Penford.

event_claims.description: Penford and Ingredion executed a Secrecy Agreement effective July 20, 2014.
event_claims.quote_text: Subsequently, Penford and Ingredion executed a Secrecy Agreement, effective as of July 20, 2014, providing for limited exchange of confidential information

event_claims.description: Executive Committee directed management to retain Deutsche Bank as financial advisor.
event_claims.quote_text: the Executive Committee directed management to proceed to retain Deutsche Bank as the Company’s financial advisor and to advise on shareholder matters and to evaluate strategic alternatives

event_claims.description: Penford received Ingredion's August 10 indication of interest at $18.00 per share in cash.
event_claims.quote_text: On August 10, 2014, Penford received a letter from Mr. Fortnum, confirming Ingredion’s interest in a possible business combination between Ingredion and the Company

event_claims.description: Party A informally discussed potential interest in acquiring or combining with Penford.
event_claims.quote_text: Party A’s Chief Executive Officer also informally discussed Party A’s potential interest in acquiring or combining with Penford.

event_claims.description: SEACOR stated it was not interested in participating in a sale process at that time.
event_claims.quote_text: Mr. Behrens stated that SEACOR was not interested in participating in any potential sale process at that time.

event_claims.description: Board directed management to proceed with further investigation and development of Ingredion's proposal.
event_claims.quote_text: the board members present at the meeting unanimously directed management to proceed with further investigation and development of the proposal from Ingredion.

event_claims.description: Penford declined to enter exclusive negotiations with Ingredion.
event_claims.quote_text: Mr. Malkoski indicated that the current status of negotiations and the proposed price did not justify providing exclusivity.

event_claims.description: Ingredion and Penford executed a nondisclosure and standstill agreement.
event_claims.quote_text: On August 21, 2014, Ingredion and Penford executed a nondisclosure and standstill agreement that superseded the prior Secrecy Agreement.

event_claims.description: The board authorized management and Deutsche Bank to proceed with the market check process.
event_claims.quote_text: The board also authorized management and Deutsche Bank to proceed with the market check process as described at the meeting.

event_claims.description: Party A expressed interest in further discussions and received an NDA draft.
event_claims.quote_text: On September 9, 2014, Party A expressed interest in having further discussions regarding a transaction and Deutsche Bank forwarded Party A a nondisclosure and standstill agreement.

event_claims.description: Party B expressed interest in further discussions and received an NDA draft.
event_claims.quote_text: Also on September 9, 2014, another strategic counterparty contacted by Deutsche Bank (referred to as Party B) expressed interest in further discussions, and Deutsche Bank forwarded a nondisclosure and standstill agreement to proceed with additional discussions with Party B.

event_claims.description: Party C expressed interest in pursuing a transaction and received an NDA draft.
event_claims.quote_text: On September 10, 2014, a third potential strategic counterparty contacted by Deutsche Bank (referred to as Party C) expressed interest in pursuing a transaction

event_claims.description: Party D expressed interest and received an NDA draft.
event_claims.quote_text: On September 11, 2014, a fourth potential strategic counterparty contacted by Deutsche Bank (referred to as Party D) expressed interest, and Deutsche Bank forwarded a nondisclosure and standstill agreement to Party D.

event_claims.description: Party B decided not to move forward with discussions or sign an NDA.
event_claims.quote_text: On September 12, 2014, Party B informed Deutsche Bank that it had decided not to move forward with discussions or sign a nondisclosure agreement with respect to a potential transaction.

event_claims.description: Deutsche Bank contacted Party E by voicemail regarding a potential transaction.
event_claims.quote_text: Also on September 12, 2014, Deutsche Bank left a voicemail for another strategic counterparty (referred to as Party E) regarding a potential transaction.

event_claims.description: Penford and Party C executed a nondisclosure and standstill agreement.
event_claims.quote_text: On September 15, 2014, Penford and Party C executed a nondisclosure and standstill agreement

event_claims.description: Ingredion indicated it could potentially increase its proposal to $18.25 or $18.50 per share.
event_claims.quote_text: Mr. Fortnum indicated during the conversation that Ingredion could potentially increase its proposal from $18.00 to $18.25 or $18.50 per share on a fully diluted basis.

event_claims.description: Penford and Party D executed a nondisclosure and standstill agreement.
event_claims.quote_text: On September 23, 2014, Penford and Party D executed a nondisclosure and standstill agreement

event_claims.description: Deutsche Bank contacted Party F regarding a potential transaction with Penford.
event_claims.quote_text: On September 24, 2014, in accordance with the board’s authorization, Deutsche Bank contacted Party F regarding a potential transaction with Penford.

event_claims.description: Penford management presented to Party C.
event_claims.quote_text: On September 25, 2014, members of Penford’s management team provided a presentation to Party C

event_claims.description: Party F declined further discussions regarding a transaction.
event_claims.quote_text: On September 29, 2014, Party F communicated to Deutsche Bank that a combination with Penford was not a suitable strategic fit and declined to pursue further discussions regarding a transaction.

event_claims.description: Penford and Party A executed a nondisclosure and standstill agreement.
event_claims.quote_text: On September 30, 2014, Penford and Party A executed a nondisclosure and standstill agreement

event_claims.description: Penford received Ingredion's revised indication of interest increasing the proposed price to $19.00 per share.
event_claims.quote_text: On October 2, 2014, Penford received a letter from Ingredion regarding its indication of interest and increasing the proposed price to $19.00 per share of outstanding Penford capital stock on a fully diluted basis.

event_claims.description: Board directed management to proceed to negotiate and finalize a definitive agreement with Ingredion.
event_claims.quote_text: the directors present at the board meeting unanimously directed management to proceed to negotiate and finalize a definitive agreement with Ingredion.

event_claims.description: Party D indicated it did not intend to move forward with discussions.
event_claims.quote_text: Party D, who indicated that it did not intend to move forward with discussions regarding a potential transaction involving the Company.

event_claims.description: Ingredion confirmed the proposed price of $19.00.
event_claims.quote_text: On October 14, 2014, Mr. Fortnum called Mr. Malkoski to confirm the proposed price of $19.00 and discuss finalizing the draft merger agreement and proceeding with the transaction as previously planned.

event_claims.description: Party A submitted a formal indication of interest at $16.00 per share.
event_claims.quote_text: Later on October 14, 2014, Party A provided a formal letter with its indication of interest with respect to an acquisition of the Company at a price of $16.00 per share

participation_count_claims.quote_text: In 2007 and 2009, the Company received unsolicited indications of interest to acquire Penford from two parties engaged in the Company’s industry.

Throughout early- to mid-September, in accordance with the board’s authorization, Deutsche Bank contacted six potential strategic counterparties in the same or similar industries to gauge initial interest regarding a potential transaction with Penford.

Deutsche Bank further reported to the board on the status of the market check process, stating that nondisclosure agreements had been executed with two potential counterparties, and that a third potential counterparty was reviewing a draft nondisclosure agreement.
